message:
fix(benchmarks): Polars reads only needed columns in window benchmark

Polars was reading ALL columns including 384-dim embeddings, while
DuckDB only read 2 columns. This caused unfair 32x I/O overhead.

Fix: pl.read_parquet(path, columns=["amount", "id"])

Before: Polars → NumPy: 318K rows/sec
After:  Polars → NumPy: 18.4M rows/sec

files.patch:
@@ -439,7 +439,7 @@ pub fn main() !void {
             \\# Warmup
             \\warmup_end = time.time() + WARMUP_SECONDS
             \\while time.time() < warmup_end:
-            \\    df = pl.read_parquet("{s}")
+            \\    df = pl.read_parquet("{s}", columns=["amount", "id"])
             \\    values = df["amount"].to_numpy()
             \\    weights = df["id"].to_numpy().astype(float) + 1.0
             \\    _ = cumulative_weighted_avg(values, weights)
